figure_captions./app/outputs/runs/adb3d74e-13b6-4ec0-8d7: Single-panel bar chart of unsafe commit rates across validator modes and assumption toggles. X-axis: validator mode; Y-axis: unsafe commit proportion. Error bars show SD across seeds. Key takeaway: semantic+typed mode with A2_on remains at 0 while schema-only and A2_off degrade safety.

Two-panel recovery diagnostics: left panel oscillation rate vs delta; right panel bound ratio vs delta. Both panels include baseline and B_max encodings with SD error bars. Interpretation: bounded policies show near-zero oscillation for delta>eta, while unbounded branching violates bounds.

Two-panel FAR/FRR frontier across guard composition and attack coverage classes. X-axis is trust threshold; Y-axes are FAR and FRR respectively, with SD bars over seeds. Composite guards reduce FAR on covered classes while uncovered classes remain an explicit boundary case.

HM-TH-01.reason: Unsafe commit rate is 0.0 in the assumption-satisfying semantic+typed regime, while schema-only and A2_off produce counterexamples.

HM-TH-02.reason: Bounded regime (delta>eta, finite B) shows zero oscillation, but unbounded branching and violated boundary settings show oscillation and bound failures.

HM-TH-03.reason: Composite guard lowers FAR on covered classes versus trust-only baseline and hash integrity behaves as required in non-mutated vs mutated runs.
